blocks.text: If the vehicle speed exceeds a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time when the accelerator pedal is depressed with contact 2 of the brake switch ON, or if deceleration/stopping is repeated a predetermined number of times after the vehicle speed exceeds a predetermined value with contact 2 of the brake switch OFF, the PGM-FI ECU determines that a failure has occurred. The PGM-FI ECU judges that a failure has occurred and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC
Gas pedal To step on the pedal.

blocks.text: If any of the following is established

items.text: Disconnection of the cord (BKSWNC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the brake switch (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Short circuit of power supply in the cord (BKSWNC line) between PGM-FI ECU and brake switch
Ground fault in cord (BKSWNC line) between PGM-FI ECU and brake switch

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the DTC and freeze data are stored without turning on the PGM-FI warning light.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
